qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service via unbounded HTTP request body memory consumption
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unbounded HTTP request body buffering through Starlette request.body() in BentoML serde/server paths. The advisory concerns symlink target validation in safe_extract_tarfile() during tar extraction, leading to arbitrary file write. The vulnerable function, data flow, and root cause do not match.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described safe_extract_tarfile() symlink path traversal root cause. The only qualifying finding is an open-world denial of service in HTTP request body parsing.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-m6w7-qv66-g3mf describes arbitrary file write via src/bentoml/_internal/utils/filesystem.py safe_extract_tarfile(), where a symlink member target is not validated and later file extraction follows the symlink outside the destination.
The qualifying finding unbounded_request_body_dos describes src/_bentoml_impl/serde.py and server request handling reading an attacker-controlled HTTP request body into memory without a size limit.
The qualifying finding does not reference tar extraction, safe_extract_tarfile(), symlink resolution, path traversal, or arbitrary file write.
